title: [InstCombine] Infer exact for lshr by cttz
summary:
The generalization evolved from a specific case (`ashr` with `cttz` on same operand) to a broader pattern: any shift (`ashr`/`lshr`) where the shift amount is `cttz` of the same operand can be marked `exact`. The key insight is that shifting right by the number of trailing zeros always clears exactly those low bits, making the shift exact regardless of direction or zero-undef behavior.

The patch extends matching to both shift types via `I.getOpcode() == Instruction::LShr || I.getOpcode() == Instruction::AShr`. It uses `m_Specific(I.getOperand(0))` to ensure the `cttz` operand matches the shifted value, capturing the original test and new cases like `test_cttz_lshr`. The `m_Value()` for the `cttz`'s `is_zero_undef` parameter allows either `true` or `false`, as exactness holds in both cases.

This approach demonstrates a reusable strategy: identify the core property (exactness from `cttz`), verify it holds under relaxed conditions (different shift opcodes, `cttz` flags), and implement using flexible pattern matching (`m_Specific`, `m_Value`) to cover variants without over-constraining.
